review_comments:
['`requestNeedsInput` is set from the view model here, but it’s never cleared when `setModel(undefined)` runs or when the view model is disposed. This can leave the context key stuck `true`, which can incorrectly keep the Cancel/stop UI and keybindings enabled for a widget with no active session. Consider explicitly setting both `requestInProgress` and `requestNeedsInput` back to `false` in the `!model` branch and in the `onDidDisposeModel` handler.', 'The name `requestInProgressOrPendingToolCall` is now misleading because the expression also includes `ChatContextKeys.requestNeedsInput` (which isn’t a “pending tool call”). Renaming this constant to match its broadened meaning (or splitting the conditions) would avoid confusion for future maintenance.', "## Pull request overview

This PR addresses the missing “Stop/Cancel” button scenario in Chat by introducing a new context key that represents when a request is paused and needs user input, and then using that context to make cancellation available in more states.

**Changes:**
- Added a new `ChatContextKeys.requestNeedsInput` context key.
- Bound and updated `requestNeedsInput` in `ChatWidget` based on the chat model’s `requestNeedsInput` observable.
- Expanded cancel/stop action context expressions to treat “needs input” similarly to “in progress” for relevant UI/keybindings.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/actions/chatContextKeys.ts | Introduces a new `requestNeedsInput` context key for command/menu enablement. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts | Wires the model’s `requestNeedsInput` into the widget’s context keys. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/actions/chatExecuteActions.ts | Updates cancel/stop gating expressions to account for `requestNeedsInput`. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings




- **Files reviewed:** 3/3 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 2


"]

[{'sha': '70bb47bc61f95b1be7de6c6941f31b9159bf4e94', 'message': "fix: exempt agent instructions from workspace subpath filter

AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md live at the workspace
root by design. The CLI harness restricts items to workspaceSubpaths
(.github, .copilot, .agents, .claude) which filtered out these root-level
agent instruction files. Now items with groupKey 'agent-instructions' are
exempt from the subpath filter, matching the existing exemption for
instructionFileFilter patterns."}, {'sha': '446d7308520822a6592b56b988a32e7feb2e0a0e', 'message': 'sessions: include AGENTS.md in instructions listing

The sessions AI customization tree view and overview were only calling
listPromptFiles(PromptsType.instructions) to discover instruction files.
However, AGENTS.md (along with CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md)
is classified as an agent type by getPromptFileType(), so it was never
returned by that call.

Fix by also calling listAgentInstructions() and merging the results
(deduplicating by URI), matching the pattern already used by the
workbench customization editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>'}]
review_comments:
["## Pull request overview

This PR fixes discovery/filtering of workspace-root agent instruction files in the AI Customization list when using the core (non-provider) harness path. It exempts items categorized as `groupKey: 'agent-instructions'` from the active harness `workspaceSubpaths` restriction so files like `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `copilot-instructions.md` aren’t incorrectly hidden.

**Changes:**
- Exempt `agent-instructions` items from the workspace subpath filter applied in the core item fetch pipeline.

['Using `this.templateDataByRequestId.get(context.element.id)` here bypasses the existing `getTemplateDataForRequestId` safety check (which verifies `templateData.currentElement?.id` matches and cleans up stale entries). During scroll/template reuse, the map can temporarily contain stale mappings, which could finalize the wrong thinking part. Prefer using the `templateData` argument (already scoped to the current row) or `getTemplateDataForRequestId(context.element.id)` to ensure the template belongs to the element being rendered.

This issue also appears on line 1784 of the same file.', "## Pull request overview

Fixes a chat rendering issue where scrolling/re-rendering older “thinking” content could incorrectly finalize (“stop”) the currently streaming thinking/subagent parts for another response.

**Changes:**
- Scope “finalize active thinking part” logic to the currently rendered element instead of iterating all rendered templates.
- Scope “finalize subagent parts” logic to the currently rendered element to avoid cross-response finalization during re-renders.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatListRenderer.ts | Narrows thinking/subagent finalization to the current element to prevent scroll re-renders from impacting other streaming responses. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatListRenderer.ts:1788**
* Same as above: direct `templateDataByRequestId.get(context.element.id)` can return a stale template that no longer corresponds to `context.element.id` during virtualization/reuse, and you already have `getTemplateDataForRequestId(...)` for validated lookups. Consider using `templateData` (current row) or the validated getter before calling `finalizeAllSubagentParts` to avoid finalizing subagent parts for the wrong response.
```
			if (context.element.isComplete && !isSubagentContent) {
				const elementTemplateData = this.templateDataByRequestId.get(context.element.id);
				if (elementTemplateData) {
					this.finalizeAllSubagentParts(elementTemplateData);
				}
```
</details>


- **Files reviewed:** 1/1 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 1

['`initContent()` hides `this.wrapper` when `!this.hasToolItems`, but the new working spinner is created even when there are no tool items. In that case the wrapper stays `display: none`, so expanding a streaming subagent with no child tool invocations (and no prompt) will still show an empty panel and won’t fix the reported issue. Ensure the wrapper is made visible when the working spinner is created/shown (or treat the spinner as content that should unhide the wrapper).', 'The new expanded-state working spinner behavior (including wrapper visibility when there are no tool items, and removal/re-show around confirmations) isn’t covered by the existing ChatSubagentContentPart tests. Adding a unit test for “streaming subagent expanded with no child tools” would help prevent regressions for this issue.', "## Pull request overview

**Changes:**

- Insert new subagent items (tools/markdown/hooks) before the spinner/result via a shared insertion anchor.

review_comments:
["`reviewStateObs` derives `sessionChanges` from `viewModel.activeSessionChangesObs`, which is affected by the UI's `ChangesVersionMode` (e.g. All Changes / Last Turn). The session code review action + toolbar contribution compute the review version from `activeSession.changes` (session changes only), so changing version mode can make the loading/comment-count indicator here disagree with the actual code review state/version.

Consider deriving review files/version from the same source as the action (session changes), e.g. inject `ISessionsManagementService` and read `activeSession.changes`, or expose a dedicated observable on `ChangesViewModel` for “session changes” that is independent of `versionModeObs`.", "## Pull request overview

Refactors the Sessions “Changes” view (`vs/sessions` layer) to move more reactive toolbar/button logic out of `ChangesViewPane` and into a dedicated widget, reducing view-pane responsibility.

**Changes:**
- Reworks `ChangesButtonBarWidget` to be a `Disposable` that internally derives outgoing-change counts and code-review state.
- Introduces a structurally-equal derived observable for review button state (`isLoading` + `commentCount`) to avoid unnecessary churn.
- Removes direct `ICodeReviewService` usage from `ChangesViewPane` by relocating code review UI state computation.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts` | Extracts/re-homes toolbar state derivations into `ChangesButtonBarWidget` and simplifies `ChangesViewPane` wiring. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings




- **Files reviewed:** 1/1 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 1


"]

review_comments:
['In `ChangesButtonBarWidget._getButtonConfiguration`, the fallback path for unknown `MenuItemAction`s with an icon now forces `isSecondary: true`. Previously, omitting `isSecondary` let `WorkbenchButtonBar` decide primary vs secondary based on index. This changes layout/priority of extension-contributed actions (e.g. the first icon-only action will always be rendered as secondary). Consider returning `{ showIcon: true, showLabel: false }` without `isSecondary`, or compute `isSecondary` based on the provided index (pass it through to `_getButtonConfiguration`).', "`renderCompressedElements` hides file-specific decorations, but it doesn’t clear the `'modified'` CSS class added to `.monaco-icon-name-container` when this template was previously used for a file element. Because tree rows/templates are virtualized and reused, this can leave folder rows styled as modified. Clear that class (and any other file-only classes) when rendering compressed folder elements (and similarly in `renderFolderElement`/`renderRootElement`).

This issue also appears on line 377 of the same file.", "## Pull request overview

This PR reorganizes the Sessions “Changes” view implementation by extracting previously inlined logic from `changesView.ts` into dedicated modules, improving separation of concerns for UI rendering and view-model state.

**Changes:**
- Extracted the changes tree renderer + tree-building helpers into `changesViewRenderer.ts`.
- Extracted the view model logic into `changesViewModel.ts`.
- Refactored `changesView.ts` to consume the new modules and introduced a `ChangesButtonBarWidget` wrapper around `MenuWorkbenchButtonBar`.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesViewRenderer.ts | New module for tree element types, tree construction, and the `ChangesTreeRenderer`. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesViewModel.ts | New module for the extracted `ChangesViewModel` and related derived observables. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts | Refactor to use new modules; introduces `ChangesButtonBarWidget` and removes inlined renderer/view-model code. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesViewRenderer.ts:381**
* `renderRootElement`/`renderFolderElement` hide the change decorations but don’t clear the `'modified'` class that `renderFileElement` may have added to `.monaco-icon-name-container` on this reused template. This can cause root/folder rows to inherit modified styling. Remove the class when rendering non-file elements.
```
		templateData.reviewCommentsBadge.style.display = 'none';

```

commits:
[{'sha': 'f78024b35a9f8d57bc7150d33b3c9db47516d807', 'message': 'Fix #297799: color picker in private fields

Update regex to avoid matching hex colors outside strings.
Prevent false positives for private fields (#foo).
Preserve valid cases like "#fff".'}]
review_comments:
['The new lookbehind `(?<=['"][ ]*)` is variable-length because of `*`, which is not supported by the JS RegExp engine and will throw a SyntaxError when this module is parsed. Please rewrite this part of the regex without variable-length lookbehind (e.g. match the quote/whitespace in the overall match and adjust the computed range accordingly, or use only fixed-length lookbehinds).', 'This regex still matches bare hex colors at the start of a line via the `^(#)([A-Fa-f0-9]{...})` alternatives. That means a private field written without indentation (e.g. `class Foo {\n#facade;\n}`) would still be detected as a color, so the false positive isn’t fully eliminated. Consider removing the start-of-line hex alternatives (to align with the PR description of only matching inside strings), or otherwise tightening the non-string matching so it can’t match valid private field syntax.
```suggestion
	const initialValidationRegex = /\b(rgb|rgba|hsl|hsla)(\([0-9\s,.\%\/]*\))|(?<=['"][ ]*)(#)([A-Fa-f0-9]{3})\b|(?<=['"][ ]*)(#)([A-Fa-f0-9]{4})\b|(?<=['"][ ]*)(#)([A-Fa-f0-9]{6})\b|(?<=['"][ ]*)(#)([A-Fa-f0-9]{8})\b/gm;
```', "The added regression test only covers a 6-hex-digit private field name (`#facade`). Since the matcher has separate branches for 3/4/6/8-digit hex, it would be good to add a couple of additional cases (e.g. `#fed`, `#fedc`, `#fedbca0`/`#fedbcaff`) and also a no-indentation variant to ensure private fields aren’t detected regardless of formatting.
```suggestion
			{ content: 'class Component {\n\treadonly #facade = inject(Facade);\n}', name: 'private field #facade with injection should not be a color' },
			{ content: 'class Foo {\n\t#fed\n}', name: 'private field #fed should not be a color' },
			{ content: 'class Foo {\n\t#fedc\n}', name: 'private field #fedc should not be a color' },
			{ content: 'class Foo {\n\t#fedbca0\n}', name: 'private field #fedbca0 should not be a color' },
			{ content: 'class Foo {\n\t#fedbcaff\n}', name: 'private field #fedbcaff should not be a color' },
			{ content: 'class Foo {\n#fed\n}', name: 'private field #fed without indentation should not be a color' },
```", '## Pull request overview

This PR adjusts the editor’s default document color detection to avoid false positives where JavaScript/TypeScript private fields (e.g. `#facade`) were being detected as hex colors, and updates the associated unit tests.

**Changes:**
- Tighten the early-validation regex for hex colors to reduce matches outside quoted strings.
- Remove an outdated test that asserted hex detection after leading whitespace.
- Add regression coverage for JS/TS private fields that resemble hex colors.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/vs/editor/common/languages/defaultDocumentColorsComputer.ts` | Updates the initial color-detection regex to avoid matching `#...` in more non-string contexts. |
| `src/vs/editor/test/common/languages/defaultDocumentColorsComputer.test.ts` | Updates tests to reflect the new matching behavior and adds a regression test for private fields. |



', '@microsoft-github-policy-service agree']
